nodes.context: Yakuza group based in Yamaguchi, Japan, largest in the prefecture.
Old yakuza clan of which Koichi Goda was a member.
Yakuza group in Yamaguchi Prefecture, second largest after Goda-ikka.
Chugoku-based yakuza organization, member of Gosha-kai.
Chugoku-based yakuza organization, member of Gosha-kai.
Chugoku-based yakuza organization, member of Gosha-kai.
Shikoku-based yakuza organization, member of Gosha-kai.

edges.evidence_quote: The group was formed in 1948 as the Goda-gumi ( 合田組 , Gōda-gumi ) in Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi by Koichi Goda, then a member of an old yakuza clan, the Kagotora-gumi.
edges.context: Goda-ikka's founder was a member of Kagotora-gumi, indicating a predecessor relationship.

edges.evidence_quote: It is the largest yakuza group in Yamaguchi Prefecture , followed by the Yamaguchi-gumi .
edges.context: Goda-ikka and Yamaguchi-gumi are rival yakuza groups in the same prefecture, with Goda-ikka being larger.

edges.evidence_quote: Since 1996, the Goda-ikka has been a member of an anti- Yamaguchi federation, the Gosha-kai, along with three other Chugoku -based organizations, the Kyosei-kai , the Kyodo-kai , the Asano-gumi , and the Shikoku -based Shinwa-kai .
edges.context: Goda-ikka is a member of the Gosha-kai, an anti-Yamaguchi federation.

edges.evidence_quote: Since 1996, the Goda-ikka has been a member of an anti- Yamaguchi federation, the Gosha-kai, along with three other Chugoku -based organizations, the Kyosei-kai , the Kyodo-kai , the Asano-gumi , and the Shikoku -based Shinwa-kai .
edges.context: Goda-ikka and Kyosei-kai are both members of the Gosha-kai, cooperating against Yamaguchi-gumi.
